title: Sea Ice Thermodynamic-Dynamic Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Sea Ice Thermodynamic-Dynamic Inversion: Sea ice state estimation: retrieve ice concentration, thickness, and drift from passive microwave and altimetry. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.passive_microwave.brightness_temp…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; melt_pond_fraction, snow_ice_interface_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: T_B(f, theta, p) = (1-A)*T_ocean + A*[T_ice + f_snow(T_snow)]; ice motion from TOPAZ/neXtSIM
E.world_state_x: sea ice concentration A (%), thickness h_i (m), drift velocity u_i (m/day)
E.observation_y: satellite brightness temperatures T_B(19,37,85 GHz), ICESat/Cryosat-2 freeboard

W.regime: Existence of the recovered sea_ice_state_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); melt_pond_fraction dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: SIC uncertainty < 5% for compact ice; thickness < 0.5 m. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |melt_pond_fraction|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_swath_pixels * N_frequencies) per orbit per iteration
